translated: Jesus told this parable to them: ‚ÄúA farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path and the birds came and ate it up.‚Äù

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu latofo disibu l…õma nwu, ni aat…îk…î ma al…õ, ‚ÄúS…õka kalek…î lele ni…õs…õ bawo di akudi akudi b…õ…õtaka bay…õ baawo suoto yi, manyila. Nkpo okle kaf…î…î se okpoo ee leyo lewo kam…õ batii l…õs…õ bawo akudi akudi b…î…îkp…õko bawo yi, mambi…õ.
translated: Jesus told this parable, saying, ‚ÄúA certain man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenants, and went away for a long time. When the time came to harvest the fruit, he sent a servant to the tenants to receive some of the fruit, but they beat him and sent him away empty-handed.

ref: Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWho is my mother and who are my brothers?‚Äù
text: Ni aakaal…õ ma al…õ, ‚ÄúBawe ninle yaami omu ku babiloo bamu?‚Äù
translated: Did you not hear them say, ‚ÄúWho will bring us back from these distant lands?‚Äù

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Yesu lat…îk…î basaateketet…õ n…î…î al…õ bata batii nwu basi…õ akudi akudi s…õ…õfa l…õk…õt…õ…õ k…înle nwu suoto.
translated: Jesus told a parable about a man who planted good seed in his field but while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Nfanwu Yesu lat…îk…î basaateketet…õ n…î…î al…õ, babuo di oklo kam…õ wla baat…î…îs…î okpoo w…î b…î…îl…õ…õ ni Betsaida di lekpo nwu s…õmaa. Ninf…õ aadiki batii dikudi nwu kaf…î…î osuku n…õ.
translated: Jesus took a boat to a quiet place to be alone, but the crowds heard about it and followed him on foot from the towns. When he landed, he saw a large crowd and had compassion on them.

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.‚Äù
text: Diele alesaa ya di otii nnle ankpee ni kaafutu kam…õ ninta al…õ ankp…în…înf…î. Kaf…î…î s…õlaa se nindie sinkye ni otii kanya kam…õ ninta otii nkp…în…înf…î.‚Äù
translated: The children went to the farm and saw that the cassava had been eaten by goats. They came back and told their mother that the goats had eaten the cassava.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.‚Äù The Final Judgment
text: Kp…õmblat…õ w…î mmle diesinl…õ ata l…õsal…õsaa …îblako, nkpo oso biy…î nw…î bifuki binyu kaayi di ditunu nwu nkpo kam…õ. Nfa abawi kaku anwee any…õ…õ n…õ. Otii Obi Nwu Nkpo Nimakanko Batii Bamuu Kaayi.
translated: The elders say that in ancient times, a certain man lived in a village, and whenever he went to the farm he would always bring back only one yam. No one ever saw him carry more. His name was Obi Who Brings Only One Yam from the Farm.
